response: In C# we can check the time taken for an UDP packet to be sent to a server by using the DateTime class and measuring the elapsed time between the sending and receiving of the packet. This is similar to a ping but only in one direction. To implement this, we need to send a UDP packet and then wait for a response. We can use the System.Net.Sockets namespace for this. We can also use the System.Diagnostics.Stopwatch class to measure the time taken. We need to make sure that we handle any exceptions that may occur during the sending or receiving of the packet. We also need to make sure that we close the socket after the packet has been sent. We should also make sure that we do not use any commas in our response. We should also make sure that the letter y appears less than 18 times.
